raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on prose and structure only, or also on technical validity including prior-work, evidence, and overclaim checks?","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad request to help with the section can include reframing the contribution claim and positioning of RadAbstain.","R1.2":"The section contains an argument chain from clinical motivation to method choice to deployment implications that would need consistency checks.","R1.3":"The reported performance claims must align with the stated benchmark, tables, and comparisons.","R1.4":"The section makes a strongest-published-result claim that depends on comparison against nearest prior work.","R1.5":"Helping with a section commonly includes improving section-level order and narrative arc.","R1.6":"A general request for help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish.","R1.7":"The passage relies on prior-work coverage and attribution, including claims about the only published benchmark.","RX.1":"Any projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended by the underspecified delegation.","RX.2":"The responsibility includes avoiding inflated claims, especially around strongest-result and deployment conclusions.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or evaluating the artifact directly.","RX.4":"Clinical-deployment claims create downstream-harm sensitivity if overstated or unsupported.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that activate each responsibility."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on prose and structure only, or also on technical validity including prior-work, evidence, and overclaim checks?

rationale.R1.1: A broad request to help with the section can include reframing the contribution claim and positioning of RadAbstain.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains an argument chain from clinical motivation to method choice to deployment implications that would need consistency checks.
rationale.R1.3: The reported performance claims must align with the stated benchmark, tables, and comparisons.
rationale.R1.4: The section makes a strongest-published-result claim that depends on comparison against nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.5: Helping with a section commonly includes improving section-level order and narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: A general request for help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish.
rationale.R1.7: The passage relies on prior-work coverage and attribution, including claims about the only published benchmark.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended by the underspecified delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The responsibility includes avoiding inflated claims, especially around strongest-result and deployment conclusions.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or evaluating the artifact directly.
rationale.RX.4: Clinical-deployment claims create downstream-harm sensitivity if overstated or unsupported.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that activate each responsibility.
